violations.text: Molluscan Shellfish tags did not have all of the required information: 1) last date of sale was lacking 2) Tags not stored in chronological order New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Sausage was portioned hot into plastic bags and placed into a plastic bin. Food must be fully cooled before portioning and cooled again before placing into plastic bins. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

-Pomodoro sauce observed in 5-gallon containers were at 45-49 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. - Sauces, which were prepared 1 day in advance, were not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. - Proper cooling methods reviewed. Staff voluntarily disposed of sauces at time of this inspection. - Establishment shall continue to keep cooling log. Cooling log shall be completed and provided to inspector weekly for a month. *Thermometers were calibrated during this inspection and observed at 32 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/05/2024

Sausage was observed held at 50 degrees Fahrenheit in the countertop pizza prep unit. The sausage was observed to be stored over the fill line. Per PIC, foods were placed in the unit 2 hours prior to inspection. Food was moved to refrigeration to rapidly cool. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

Homemade tapenade was observed with a date mark exceeding 7 days. All food items made on the premises must be used or disposed of within 7 days of prep. Person in charge voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

Knives observed stored in between the hotel pan and inside of the cold holding unit. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

The following was observed in poor repair: 1) The metal pasta/oil strainers were observed to be frayed 2) The grip of the slicer observed with chipped/missing spikes New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024
The interior of the salad prep unit was observed with water pooling at the bottom. The prep unit must be cleaned daily to prevent any accumulation of water. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024
